preamble: The vocalization of a glottal stop into a neutral schwa [ə] represents a form of lenition where the consonantal closure is lost, leaving behind a default mid-central vowel.

examples.note: Vocalization of the neutral laryngeal into the 'schwa primum'.
Weakening of Aleph often results in a reduced 'shva' vowel or helper vowel.

sources: The Phonology and Morphology of Biblical Hebrew (Garr, 2014)
